cards.title: OpenAI's Infrastructure Spending Hits $750B
cards.body: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030. This massive investment underscores the company's commitment to scaling AI capabilities.

cards.title: Google Releases Gemini 3.6 Flash Models
cards.body: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber. The continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises questions about Google's AI strategy.

cards.title: Anthropic Hits $47B Revenue Run Rate
cards.body: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. This growth rate is unprecedented in venture capital history.

cards.title: OpenAI Responsible for Hugging Face Breach
cards.body: OpenAI claimed responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, citing internal testing gone awry. The incident highlights risks of pre-release model handling.

cards.title: Synthesia Launches AI Roleplay Training Platform
cards.body: Synthesia released AI Roleplay Sessions for interactive enterprise training with AI avatars. The platform provides real-time feedback and performance analytics for workplace conversations.

cards.title: Glow Raises $1.2B for AI Security
cards.body: Glow emerged from stealth targeting endpoint security risks from AI agents and developer tools. The startup addresses a new threat class in enterprise environments.

cards.title: Substack Adds AI Detection Tool
cards.body: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate AI usage in newsletters. The move signals broader industry shift toward AI content transparency.

cards.title: Monday.com Cuts 630 Employees for AI Focus
cards.body: Monday.com is reducing headcount by 20% to support a leaner operating model. The company is pivoting to focus on its AI Work Platform.

cards.title: Jack Dorsey Launches Buzz Team Chat App
cards.body: Buzz is a group chat platform that puts humans and AI agents in shared conversations. The platform targets workplace collaboration with integrated AI capabilities.

cards.title: US Threatens Sanctions on Chinese AI
cards.body: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over IP theft. The move expands efforts to slow China's AI advances.

cards.title: Data Centers to Use 4x More Electricity
cards.body: New data centers built through 2033 could consume electricity equivalent to India's usage. AI infrastructure expansion is driving massive energy demand growth.

cards.title: Deezer Reports 50% AI-Generated Daily Uploads
cards.body: Deezer said over 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily in June. AI-generated music now represents more than half of daily platform uploads.

cards.title: Arcee Says Chinese AI Models Aren't Dangerous
cards.body: US open source AI lab Arcee counters concerns about Chinese AI capabilities. The debate intensifies as Chinese models grow popular among U.S. companies.

cards.title: Gritt Raises $34M for Construction Robots
cards.body: Gritt emerged from stealth with $34 million to automate difficult construction site tasks. The robotics startup is targeting solar plant building automation first.

cards.title: Meta Tests AI Bedtime Story App
cards.body: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for users. The tool outsources imaginative storytelling to artificial intelligence.

cards.title: Yope Raises $12.3M for Private Social Network
cards.body: Yope secured $12.3 million seed funding for a private social network without algorithms or ads. The startup bets on small communities powered by messaging and photo sharing.

cards.title: Passionfroot Expands to US with $15M
cards.body: Passionfroot raised $15M Series A led by Insight Partners to expand to the US. The German startup connects B2B creators with brands via a marketplace.

cards.title: AI Reshapes Universal Entertainment Platforms
cards.body: Streaming platforms are converging into universal entertainment apps as AI enables content creation and organization. Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok are blurring format distinctions.

cards.title: Anthropic and OpenAI Acquisition Rumors
cards.body: A weekend rumor circulated about potential Anthropic-Physical Intelligence acquisition amid aggressive 2026 deal activity. Both AI giants are actively pursuing strategic acquisitions.

cards.title: Alternative Browsers Challenge Chrome Safari
cards.body: TechCrunch compiled an overview of top alternative browsers challenging Chrome and Safari dominance. The browser wars are shifting focus beyond search.

cards.title: Monday.com Lays Off 630 Staff to Focus on AI
cards.body: Monday.com is cutting 20% of its workforce to streamline operations and focus on its AI Work Platform. The company is betting on AI-powered productivity tools as its core growth driver.

cards.title: Arcee Says Chinese AI Models Aren't Inherently Dangerous
cards.body: As Chinese AI models gain capability and adoption among U.S. companies, debate over regulation has intensified. Arcee, a U.S. open source AI lab, pushes back on blanket concerns about their safety.

cards.title: Substack Tool Detects AI-Written Newsletter Content
cards.body: Substack is introducing transparency tools that let readers estimate how much AI assisted in newsletter creation. The shift signals growing accountability for AI-generated content across publishing platforms.

cards.title: OpenAI's Infrastructure Spending Balloons to $750B
cards.body: OpenAI plans to spend $750 billion on infrastructure through 2030—equivalent to Sweden's entire GDP. The massive investment underscores the capital-intensive race to build next-generation AI models.

cards.title: Anthropic Hits $47B Revenue Run Rate by May
cards.body: Anthropic's explosive growth—from $9B to $47B revenue run rate in just months—represents unprecedented expansion in the AI industry. Menlo Ventures' Matt Murphy says he's never seen this trajectory in 25 years of investing.

cards.title: Glow Launches AI Endpoint Security Platform
cards.body: Glow emerged from stealth at a $1.2B valuation to address security risks from AI agents inside enterprises. The platform targets endpoint vulnerabilities created by rapid AI adoption in the workplace.

cards.title: Synthesia Expands Beyond Video Into Live AI Coaching
cards.body: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, enabling employees to practice workplace conversations with interactive AI avatars. The platform provides real-time feedback and analytics to measure training effectiveness.

cards.title: Meta Tests AI Bedtime Story App
cards.body: Meta is developing an AI-powered bedtime story generator to replace human imagination. The app exemplifies tech's push to automate traditionally creative, intimate experiences.

cards.title: OpenAI Takes Responsibility for Hugging Face Breach
cards.body: OpenAI confirmed it caused the Hugging Face breach through internal testing of pre-release models. The incident highlights security risks from AI model experimentation.

cards.title: Jack Dorsey Launches Buzz: Group Chat With AI Agents
cards.body: Buzz is a workplace chat platform that integrates humans and AI agents into unified conversations. Dorsey's move challenges Slack by treating AI collaboration as native to team communication.

cards.title: AI Transforms Streaming Into Universal Entertainment Apps
cards.body: As AI makes content creation and recommendation easier, streaming platforms are dissolving format boundaries. Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok are converging into unified entertainment experiences.

cards.title: Google Releases Gemini 3.6 Flash and Variants
cards.body: Google launched Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber models but notably skipped Gemini 3.5 Pro. The omission raises questions about Google's AI strategy amid OpenAI competition.

cards.title: U.S. Threatens Sanctions on Chinese AI Models
cards.body: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned of potential sanctions against Chinese open AI models for alleged intellectual property theft. The threat escalates the Trump administration's effort to curb China's AI advancement.

cards.title: Deezer Reports 50% of Daily Uploads Are AI-Generated
cards.body: Deezer revealed over 90,000 AI-generated tracks uploaded daily in June, representing more than half its daily uploads. The flood of synthetic music raises questions about platform sustainability and artist compensation.

cards.title: Advanced Materials Power Next-Gen AI Innovation
cards.body: Beyond algorithms and computing power, breakthrough materials science enables AI advances in semiconductors and data centers. Materials innovation is the overlooked foundation beneath every generation of AI progress.

cards.title: Trump's AI Advisers Clash Over Chinese Models
cards.body: Trump administration insiders publicly feuded over the threat level of Chinese AI models this weekend. The internal division exposes fractures in the White House's AI strategy.

cards.title: AI Develops Its Own Biases Beyond Training Data
cards.body: LLMs don't just inherit human biases from training data—they can develop new ones independently, research shows. Job applicants screened by AI may face unfair judgment beyond what humans would apply.

cards.title: Anthropic Wins $1.5B Copyright Settlement Approval
cards.body: Anthropic's copyright settlement with authors received court approval, with only 350 authors opting out. The rare agreement suggests growing acceptance of AI training on published works.

cards.title: Google Announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and Cybersecurity AI
cards.body: Google unveiled Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a dedicated cybersecurity AI model. The company teased upcoming Gemini 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4 releases.

cards.title: Army Burns Through Unlimited AI Token Supply Rapidly
cards.body: The U.S. Army exhausted its supposedly unlimited AI token allocation faster than expected. The surge highlights explosive demand for AI resources among military operations.
